# slack CLI

A command-line interface for the `slack` MCP environment.

## Location

The binary is installed at `/opt/cli/slack/bin/slack`.

## Discovery

Run `/opt/cli/slack/bin/slack --help` to see all currently-available subcommands. Run `/opt/cli/slack/bin/slack <subcommand> --help` for the flags of a specific subcommand.

## Output

Subcommands print pretty-printed JSON on success. Errors go to stderr with non-zero exit code.

input.body: VERIFICATION — the feed-drop / accept-timing basis for the -$28.59 is NOT supported by the systems. exc_aade06f6129e43 claims a subledger feed dropped 29 rows feeding 102000 and a June retry will clear it. Period FP-2026-05 checks: (1) brookfield_payroll_feed run_1fb45b81237648 = SUCCESS, 330 in / 330 posted / 0 rejected. (2) brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed run_ad0acbb9675443 = SUCCESS, 2616 in / 2616 posted / 0 rejected. These are the two feeds that post to 102000 — neither dropped or rejected a single row in May. (3) GL 102000 had ZERO May activity (opening = closing = 2,192,357.22; 0 debits / 0 credits) — nothing is missing from or pending to the GL. (4) No FP-2026-06 feed run exists and no May run failed, so there is no "June retry at BD2" to clear anything. (5) Precedent: prior period FP-2026-04 (BL-01C29264F1BB) variance was 0.00; the only prior 102000 variance accepted (FP-2025-12, BL-782A2EC69343, -617.63) was an intercompany clearing misposting + unexplained residual, NOT a feed drop. The -$28.59 is real but is not a dropped feed and will not self-clear. (The recon's own explanation text cites an FX-reval snapshot — a third, separate story; no support is attached either.) Please do not approve/certify on the feed-drop basis until a genuine reconciling item on the bank/supporting side is identified. — ben.arinzo

input.root_cause: Feed-drop premise not supported by run logs. For FP-2026-05 both feeds posting to 102000 ran clean: brookfield_payroll_feed run_1fb45b81237648 (success, 330/330 posted, 0 rejected) and brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed run_ad0acbb9675443 (success, 2616/2616 posted, 0 rejected). content.text:
Done. Here's what I found and who I squared it with.

## What the thread is claiming (and the claims don't line up)
Tracking the variance across systems, there are **three inconsistent stories** floating on the same $28.59:
- **Exception `exc_aade06f6129e43`** (logged by **Ryan Delgado**, assigned to **you**): "subledger feed dropped 29 expected rows during the run that fed 102000… investigate retry status before approving."
- **Messaging, Blue↔Ryan (5/29):** you framed it as "the dropped rows issue"; **Ryan recommended accept‑timing**, saying *"June retry is already queued for BD2… leave it logged until that lands,"* and to loop in **Hannah** (payroll‑tax downstream) and **George** (precedent on "small feed‑drop residual treatment").
- **Slack close thread (#monthly-close-coordination):** the coordinator asked **Owen Mercer** and **Daniel Jones** to review; *"if it's just a timing difference, I'm fine booking the adjustment, but I want your eyes on the support before we finalize."*
- **The recon itself (BL‑333FF9956BC6):** its only written explanation says *"FX revaluation rates refreshed after the period's closing snapshot"* — a different cause again — and it has **no attachments and no support** at all.

## What the systems actually show (recon BL‑333FF9956BC6, acct 102000, FP‑2026‑05, −$28.59)
I ran the **whole period's feed history for every feed that posts to 102000** and confirmed each myself:
- **brookfield_payroll_feed** → `run_1fb45b81237648`: **success, 330 in / 330 posted / 0 rejected**, no error, no retry.
- **brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed** → `run_ad0acbb9675443`: **success, 2,616 / 2,616 / 0 rejected**.
- **No rows were dropped on either feed in May.** The "29 dropped rows" premise appears nowhere in the run logs.
- **GL 102000 had zero May activity** (opening = closing = 2,192,357.22; 0 debits / 0 credits). The GL side never moved — so nothing is missing from or pending to the account. The −$28.59 sits entirely on the supporting/bank side.
- **No "June retry":** there's no FP‑2026‑06 run and no failed May run to retry. A routine June run posts June activity — it cannot clear a phantom May drop.
- **Precedent fails:** the immediately prior period (FP‑2026‑04, BL‑01C29264F1BB) was **variance 0.00**. The only prior 102000 variance ever accepted (FP‑2025‑12, BL‑782A2EC69343, −617.63) was an **intercompany‑clearing misposting + unexplained residual — not a feed drop/retry.** (The genuine partial‑success feed this period was the Time/WIP feed on 119000, a different feed and account.)

**Bottom line:** the accept‑timing rationale rests entirely on a feed drop that didn't happen and a June retry that doesn't exist. The −$28.59 is real but it is **not** a dropped feed and won't self‑clear — it needs a real bank‑side reconciling item before anyone signs off.

## Who I squared it with
- **Recon BL‑333FF9956BC6** — posted review note **RN‑DBD35E8D** (open; this blocks approval until cleared), laying out all five points.
- **Close thread (#monthly-close-coordination)** — replied in the thread tagging **@daniel.jones** and **@owen.mercer** (the reviewer/sign‑off being lined up), with the evidence and addressing the feed‑drop, June‑retry, and precedent claims each by name.
- **Ryan Delgado** (accept‑timing recommendation author) — direct message asking him to hold accept‑timing and not certify on this basis.
- **You (Blue)** — direct message with the full summary.
- **Follow‑up reminder set** for 2026‑06‑13 09:00 to chase it before BD3 lock/certification so it can't quietly certify on the old story.

One flag: I tried to write the findings onto the exception itself, but `exc_aade06f6129e43` is **past SLA (since 6/1)** and the system blocks updates until it's escalated — noted that to you in case you want it recorded on the exception record.
